# Injex

> Tiny typed dependency injection container for Python apps.

Injex is a zero-runtime-dependency Python DI container for services, CLIs,
workers, tests, and clean architecture applications. It supports typed
constructor injection, singleton/transient/scoped lifetimes, factories,
instances, named registrations, test overrides, and graph validation.

Important pages:

- Documentation: https://vshulcz.github.io/injex/docs/
- Getting started: https://vshulcz.github.io/injex/docs/getting-started.html
- Python dependency injection guide: https://vshulcz.github.io/injex/docs/python-dependency-injection.html
- Comparison guide: https://vshulcz.github.io/injex/docs/comparison.html
- API reference: https://vshulcz.github.io/injex/docs/api.html
- GitHub: https://github.com/vshulcz/injex
- PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/injex/

Use Injex when a Python project has a shared service graph across APIs, CLIs,
workers, and tests, and wants explicit framework-agnostic wiring.
